sections: Canonical artifacts, renderer-derived views, and Projections
Intake record and artifact candidate contracts
PR-009 — Charter questionnaire-shaped coverage and posture validation
complete selected dependency and transition record

repo_truth: Fresh read-only review of the exact thirteen-entry HCM-2.2 twice-remediated planning subject. The subject is planning-only and no HCM-2.2 product or definition byte has landed. Independently revalidate the whole packet, contract fixtures, review evidence, live definitions/carriers, and executable proof boundary. Inherit no clean conclusion.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay the sorted thirteen-entry manifest and aggregate fingerprint before review.
Review every byte of 00, 04, 06, both immutable prior dispatches, HCM-2.2 SPEC, both contract fixtures, plan, unchecked todo, planning proof, and both remediation records.
Read unchanged 01, 02, 03, 05, 07, 08, HCM-0.6 decision, selected HCM-2.1 record/packet/proof, exact definition assets, and live engine/compiler/flow/CLI/skill/template/test sources only as needed.

Run bounded read-only manifest, Markdown/link, Git, handoff/schema, uniform-definition/exact-ref, source/API feasibility, retained-field/coverage, lineage/authority/transaction/lifecycle, byte-contract, cross-platform, determinism, security, bridge, and classification checks.
Return findings first with exact file/line evidence through the built-in channel.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user task hops.
No Rust, Cargo, production definitions, runtime fixtures/tests/product/generated-skill/pipeline, HCM-2.3+, Phase-3, SDK, Tauri, Substrate, contract, dock, or publication implementation.

Do not invent an alternate schema, coverage set, fingerprint producer, authority/authentication claim, transaction visibility, lifecycle, renderer, migration, compatibility mode, bridge, or sibling-family scope.
Do not treat prior findings/remediation, baseline tests, literal fixtures, or contingent status as proof that the thirteen-entry subject is clean.
tasks: Invoke required skills in order, remain read-only, replay the exact thirteen-entry manifest/aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any byte differs.
Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order with exact file/line, violated authority, failure, smallest remediation, and missing or invalidated proof.
Verify the one RFC 8785 definition producer, complete exact-ref graph, retained validator compatibility, acyclicity, old-byte preservation, and correct owner selection for every definition.
Verify sixteen non-overlapping homogeneous coverage rows, complete canonical field/validation preservation, one leaf-source mapping, value-versus-waiver authority, and all-mode equivalence.

Verify create/amend basis, approval trust, promotion/lifecycle journal, total state/event/precedence and immutable record lineage, reader visibility, recovery, and platform refusal are exact and non-circular.

Verify explicit canonical key order and raw fixture, renderer matrix/raw fixture, hashes, decision-record disabled behavior, C03/C04, product cutover, bridges, proof wall, and deletion gates are total.
Verify planning-only status, exact scope, unchecked todo, gate ceiling, CRITICAL impact warning, primary commit, and separate parent handoff/ledger closeout.
Return CLEAN only if no valid Critical or Required finding remains; otherwise CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest/aggregate, required authority, live definitions/source, literal fixtures, or mandatory read-only verification cannot be reproduced.

Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than selecting unspecified semantic bytes, fingerprint/ref behavior, authority, transaction/lifecycle visibility, unsafe fallback, compatibility, sibling, or later-slice behavior.
Return to the parent if valid remediation would require implementation now, released-definition mutation, broader product/default authority, HCM-2.3+, or global handoff/ledger mutation.
